title: T-089 — UI: TEC-052 Cost Import from D365 spec-driven screen
prompt:
# T-089 — UI: TEC-052 Cost Import from D365 (spec-driven)

Implement the MVP UI task for TEC-052 Cost Import from D365. Exact canonical prototype is missing, so this is a spec-driven UI task: `docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md` §11.5 / optional D365 cost diff import and `prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md` are authoritative; adjacent Technical prototype elements may be reused only as layout/primitives, not as a false 1:1 source. Route target: `/technical/costs/d365-import`. Apply 03-TECHNICAL red-lines (FG/WIP, factory_specs, shared BOM SSOT, Quality lab ownership, D365 optional) where applicable. Closeout requires screenshot/artifacts plus Playwright traces/artifacts per shared UI policy.

pipeline_inputs.description: UI: TEC-052 Cost Import from D365 spec-driven screen
pipeline_inputs.details:
Spec-driven because no exact D365 cost import prototype exists. Implement optional D365 cost pull screen: disabled-state banner, import job trigger if enabled, diff preview of current vs incoming cost, percent delta/source/idempotency columns, batch confirm with audit note. D365 is optional and never source of truth for factory release.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Given closeout is prepared, screenshots/artifacts and Playwright traces/artifacts where applicable are listed per shared UI policy.
- Given D365 integration is disabled, UI shows local cost history remains source of truth and import is unavailable/optional, not blocking factory release.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Given the UI surface is implemented, when compared with the dedicated layout-primitive prototype `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx:551-648` (`cost_import_d365_screen` D365-enabled diff table with Δ% colour-coding + sign-off ReasonInput on |Δ|≥5% + D365-disabled banner that keeps the rest of Technical usable), then it satisfies structural parity, visual parity, and interaction parity against the PRD §11.5 / UX-spec D365 cost diff import layout/flow (PRD/UX is canonical, prototype is layout-primitive only), captures Playwright screenshots and traces, passes axe checks, and applies the Technical red-line overlay: FG not FA, WIP/intermediate not PR-code, factory_spec/internal_product_spec, shared BOM SSOT, and D365 optional only. D365 disabled state must show local cost history (TEC-050) remains source of truth and import is not blocking factory release; applied import never overwrites local cost history in place — it appends a new cost_entry tagged source='d365'.
Given TEC-052 renders, then it satisfies the named PRD/UX spec as source of truth and does not claim exact prototype parity.
Given adjacent prototype primitives are reused, then screen semantics/routes/fields/states remain those required by PRD/UX.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given closeout is prepared, screenshots/artifacts and Playwright traces/artifacts where applicable are listed per shared UI policy.; Given D365 integration is disabled, UI shows local cost history remains source of truth and import is unavailable/optional, not blocking factory release.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED RTL/Playwright tests for PRD/UX spec-driven structure and interactions.
Run the relevant UI test for TEC-052; capture screenshot and trace artifacts.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not block on missing prototype; use spec-driven contract explicitly.
Do not use adjacent prototype as 1:1 source.
